articles.title: Starbucks workers strike over new dress code
articles.summary: Starbucks workers are striking over a new dress code requiring employees to wear black clothing under their aprons, a move the union, Starbucks Workers United, claims distracts from more crucial issues like fair pay and staffing. Starbucks defends the policy as part of a broader "Back to Starbucks" strategy by CEO Brian Niccol to refresh the brand's image and streamline operations. The company states the protests have affected less than 1% of its stores and has offered two company-branded T-shirts to employees.
